title: The MACU Report — Episode 19 — "Extremely Mild"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip (animate), do NOT first-frame. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

title_assets.the_feast: episodes/<slug>/titles/the_feast.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes house-style SCAVENGER'S FEAST card; flag for build)

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

earl.core: a hopeful wary ordinary man in a patched coat, nervous hopeful smile

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, lounging on a reclined dental chair like a throne, big grin

naturalist.core: a gaunt weathered naturalist in a faded field jacket, half-lit at the edge of a ruined kitchen, watching intently

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed in front of a chained doorway, gold chain, theatrical posing

rose.core: a sweet elderly woman with soft white hair in a floral apron and pearls, beaming, stirring an enormous pot in a ruined concrete bunker kitchen

seth.core: a nervous sweating gaunt man in a frayed coat, gap-toothed, glancing fearfully to the side, dim

sykes.core: a loud effusive grateful man in a worn jacket, hand on his chest, emotional

vonda.core: a preening moved middle-aged woman in salvaged finery, a laminated nametag pinned to her coat, hand on her chest

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. On the eve of the feast, I'll say it plainly: I did this. Four hundred frightened, scattered people — I found every one of them and brought them to safety, under one roof. I saved them. Me. After two years of nobody listening, I finally did something that mattered.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: You did, Ron. Four hundred. The record. You're a hero. Everyone's right where they need to be.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: You moved four hundred people into a locked hall the night before a banquet. History will remember it as... something.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: The hall has a maximum occupancy of four hundred. You found exactly four hundred people.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Before tomorrow's feast, our sponsor's offering every guest a complimentary pre-banquet dental check. Dr. Goldtooth, working the line.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Open up, baby — ahh — OH. OH, that's a premium row right there. Look at this man. Gold-ready. You, sweetheart — say ahh — mm, little work needed, but the molars? Investment grade. Cavity. Cavity. CAREER. Baby, you got a CAREER back here. The career ones always look so hopeful. That's the best part.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Free dental for everyone I brought in. See, Walter? That's what community gets you. Perks.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Four hundred mouths, all in one room, all checked and counted. Doc's gonna eat good tomorrow. ...Soft foods. But GOOD. And I don't know who sealed up the staff entrance, but I appreciate the professionalism. Real clean operation. Very. Clean.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: A note for the record: tomorrow's event has been fully catered. There will be no menu. The guests are not expected to order anything.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: EH — back in line, baby. NOBODY skips the check. You try to slip out the night before the feast? OHHH, I will turn you INSIDE OUT and use you as the WELCOME MAT. Folks gonna wipe their FEET on what's LEFT of your AMBITION. We are all stayin'. We are all stayin' TOGETHER. The cream of the crop does NOT leave early. OHHH yeahhh.

cues.speaker: SYKES
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Before the big day, I wanted the people I rescued to say a few words. Come on up. Don't be shy.

cues.speaker: SYKES
cues.vo: Ron. I was alone out there. Nobody came for me. You did. You gave me a place at the table. I will never forget it.

cues.speaker: VONDA
cues.vo: I have never felt so SEEN. So... selected. Out of everyone, Ron looked at me and said, "you. I want you there." Do you know what that does for a girl?

cues.speaker: EARL
cues.vo: Best week of my life, Ron. I used to be scared of crowds. Now I sleep GREAT — deep, the second my head hits the pillow. I don't know if it's the air in here or what they put in the water, but I have never slept so hard in my life. Looking forward to tomorrow.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Earl is sixty-one years old. He is survived by a daughter in Peoria and a very small dog named Biscuit.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: This. This right here. This is what it was all for.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And to Scavenger's Feast — where tonight's celebrity guest narrates Chef Rose's preparations. The Naturalist.

cues.speaker: THE NATURALIST
cues.vo: And here, as the light fails, the herd gathers — four hundred strong — drawn by warmth, by the promise of plenty. They do not run. They have never needed to run. That is the genius of the gathering. They arrive, as they always do... grateful.

cues.speaker: THE NATURALIST
cues.vo: Observe how they settle — shoulders loose, breathing slow. A creature that senses no danger never toughens. It softens. Nature's little courtesy... to the patient.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: The table is set, MACU. Four hundred places, every one. I haven't slept in two days, but I feel wonderful. I always do, the night before. Knowing that tomorrow, every single one of them gets exactly what they came for.

cues.speaker: THE NATURALIST
cues.vo: She prepares the substrate. To the herd, comfort and context have always been the same thing.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Mm — that's lovely, Rose. What's the herb?

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Rosemary, dear. For remembrance.

cues.speaker: THE NATURALIST
cues.vo: She seasons for memory.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: I put a little extra in yours, dear. You're going to want to remember this one.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Four hundred places. She set one for every single person I saved. That's care, Walter.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And helping Rose serve tomorrow — the Glowboys have volunteered for hospitality. They've been practicing. Standing behind the chairs. Very still. Very attentive.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: They've also asked that guests remain seated throughout the meal. For the ambiance. They were very clear about the seated part.

cues.speaker: SETH
cues.vo: Four hundred places, Ron — and not one set for a cook. No staff. No kitchen crew coming. There's just... us. All of us. Set out.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...That's community, Walter. That's all I ever wanted to build.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: And that makes it official, Ron — four hundred souls. Quota reac

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Headcount sufficient. Condition optimal. Commence

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: sorry. I got a little carried away. ...You've done everything right, Ron.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Hm. Your watch said something a little odd just then. "Commence." Flat little voice. I had an uncle who did that. Just the one word, out of place, at dinner. He was fine. The dinner was fine. Anyway.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's just what it sounds like when something's proud of me, Walter. I'd know. Nothing's been proud of me in two years. I learned the sound fast.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And your forecast, MACU. For the first time in the recorded history of this broadcast, the condition rating is not mild. It is not remarkably mild. It is EXTREMELY mild. Not a cloud. Not a threat. Not one breeze out of place. Perfect. For tomorrow. No further commentary at this time.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. Tomorrow: the feast. Four hundred souls, gathered by my own two hands, safe under one roof. I spent two years being right and being ignored. ...I never knew this is what being right was supposed to feel like. Turns out it feels like this. I want all four hundred of them to know I did this for them. Every name on that list. I hope they think about me tomorrow. I think they will. Goodnight.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Goodnight, Ron. Tomorrow you won't need me anymore. You've done... everything I needed. ...Sweet dreams.
